title: 10 best herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for difficulty eating are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate eating difficulties, such as dysphagia or anorexia, by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving appetite. The best essential oils for this issue include Zingiber officinale, Cinnamomum verum, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Syzygium aromaticum, Cuminum cyminum, Ginkgo biloba, Piper nigrum, and Cinnamomum zeylanicum. Without treatment, difficulty eating can lead to malnutrition, weight loss, and decreased quality of life, causing emotional distress and social isolation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing and calming properties. It has a gentle, anti-inflammatory effect on the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with eating. The oil also has a natural lubricating quality that can help to ease digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating. As a result, it can provide relief from discomfort and make eating a more enjoyable experience for those who struggle with difficulty eating.
remedy_properties: Sotheoic and anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root's sotheoic and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help soothe irritated throats and digestive tracts, making it easier to eat and digest food.

Antacid and anti-ulcer properties: The same compounds found in licorice root can also help neutralize stomach acid and protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus and stomach, reducing discomfort and pain associated with eating.

Adaptogenic and anti-stress properties: Licorice root has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce stress-related digestive issues, making it easier to eat and digest food when feeling anxious or stressed.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe digestive issues related to difficulty eating.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are used to make essential oils that have been traditionally used to calm stomach upset and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh licorice root extract from glycyrrhiza glabra plants with 100g of dried roots per batch.
Dry the harvested licorice root extract in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried licorice root extract using a copper still and 500ml of distilled water at 70°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment within 30 minutes.
Transfer the filtered oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with difficulty eating because of its natural digestive properties. When used aromatically or topically, it can stimulate digestion, alleviate nausea, and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. This can make eating a more enjoyable experience for individuals who struggle with digestive issues, allowing them to savor their food without discomfort or pain. Additionally, ginger's warming properties can also help to ease swallowing and improve appetite, making mealtimes less stressful and more enjoyable.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger's digestive stimulant property helps to increase gastric motility, reducing nausea and indigestion that can make eating uncomfortable.
Anti-Inflammatory Agent: Ginger's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, making it easier to eat and digest food without discomfort or pain.

Spasmolytic Property: Ginger's spasmolytic property helps to relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, easing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders that can make eating difficult.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the ginger plant, which contain the characteristic spicy flavor and aroma, are often used to create essential oils that help alleviate digestive issues and ease difficulties with eating.

Barks: The outer layer of the rhyzome's surface is rich in bioactive compounds that can be extracted into an essential oil, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving digestion, making it easier to eat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale plants in the morning for maximum oil yield.
Wash and peel the harvested ginger roots with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the peeled ginger roots in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginger roots with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 for 2-3 hours.
Filter and collect the extracted essential oil and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with difficulty eating because of its warming and soothing properties, which can alleviate digestive discomforts and reduce inflammation. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds work to calm upset stomachs, easing digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's natural sweetness can stimulate appetite and improve food digestion, making it easier to enjoy meals without discomfort. This gentle yet effective essential oil can bring relief to individuals struggling with eating due to digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve appetite, making it easier for individuals to enjoy their meals without discomfort or nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon essential oil may help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and gas that can make eating difficult, providing relief from discomfort.

Antispasmodic: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that may occur during meals, making it easier to eat without discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help alleviate digestive issues and soothe the digestive tract, making it easier to eat.

Barks: Containing a high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, the barks have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help treat stomach infections and reduce inflammation related to difficulty eating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark at dawn when the oil content is highest for optimal extraction.
Dry the harvested bark in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours.
Steam distill the dried cinnamomum verum bark with 10% water and 90% steam for 4-6 hours to release the essential oil.
Filter the resulting oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment within 30 minutes of distillation.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from light and heat, at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with difficulty eating because it has natural digestive properties that can ease discomfort and pain associated with swallowing. The oil's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, making it easier for food to pass through the digestive system without obstruction. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and relax the muscles in the esophagus, allowing for smoother passage of food and reducing discomfort during meals.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Fennel essential oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing symptoms like bloating and discomfort, making it easier to eat and digest food.

Spasmolytic: The anti-spasmodic properties of fennel essential oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that may be causing eating difficulties or discomfort during meals.

Anti-Inflammatory: Fennel essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to eating difficulties such as swelling, pain, or stiffness in the mouth, throat, or digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing discomfort while eating.

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anise oil, which has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders that can make eating difficult.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning for maximum potency and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Steam distill 200g of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in 1l of water for 2 hours using a copper still.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the distillation residue through sedimentation and filtration processes within 30 minutes.
Bottle the clear, pale yellow essential oil without additives or preservatives for immediate use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing and calming properties that can ease digestive discomfort. Its gentle anti-inflammatory qualities can reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making it easier to digest food. Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in chamomile essential oil can help relax the muscles of the esophagus and digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort that may accompany difficulty eating.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: Chamomile essential oil's anxiolytic properties can calm the mind and reduce stress, which is a common underlying factor in difficulty eating and swallowing disorders such as dysphagia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with difficulty eating.
Flowers: Containing azulene, a calming compound that reduces inflammation and eases symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and abdominal discomfort related to eating difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning after dew has dried off.
Rinse the harvested flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed matricaria chamomilla flowers using a copper still at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles within an hour of distillation.
Store the herbal essential oil in a cool dry place for up to six months or freeze it for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava essential oils helps with difficulty eating because its unique properties can calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany disordered eating. The oil's sedative effects can also ease digestive issues, such as bloating and discomfort, which may be triggered by certain foods or eating habits. By promoting relaxation and comfort, kava essential oils can help individuals approach mealtimes with a more positive and peaceful mindset, making it easier to enjoy their food and maintain a healthy relationship with eating.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Kava essential oil has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related difficulties in eating by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Muscle relaxant property: Kava essential oil's muscle relaxant properties can help soothe digestive spasms, cramps, and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), making it easier to digest food and eat comfortably.

Anti-inflammatory property: Kava essential oil's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to difficulties in eating by reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and discomfort.

Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the kava plant, rhyzomes contain unique compounds that aid digestion and relieve symptoms of eating difficulties such as nausea and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava roots and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned kava roots in a warm, dry place for three days.
Macerate 100 grams of dried kava roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks, shaking the container daily for optimal extraction.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool place.

Steep the mixture at room temperature for two weeks, shaking daily to facilitate infusion and solvent extraction.
Filter the resulting liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, then store the essential oil in dark glass bottle for up to one year.

Chop the dried carum carvi into small pieces and transfer them to a glass jar with a lid.
Steam distill the chopped herb in a clevenger apparatus with 5 gallons of water for 2 hours and collect the oil.
Filter the extracted oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and store it in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with difficulty eating because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, alleviating symptoms such as indigestion, bloating, and discomfort. supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat difficulty eating with herbal essential oils is by blending peppermint oil with ginger oil. remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 kg of cinnamomum verum leaves and stems, making sure to choose the freshest and highest quality material available.

Combine the cinnamomum verum leaves and stems with the carrier oil in a large glass jar, stir well to ensure even distribution, let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discard the solids and reserve the liquid, transfer it to a clean glass container.
Store the homemade cinnamon oil infusion in a cool dark place, use within 6 months and use it in moderation due to its potency.

remedy_parts: Seeds: Cuminum cyminum seeds are commonly used for essential oil production because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, particularly carvone, which has been traditionally used to aid digestion and alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating.

Leaves: Cuminum cyminum leaves are also used for essential oil production, as they contain a variety of volatile oils that have been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and difficulty eating.

Buds: Cuminum cyminum buds are occasionally used for essential oil production, as they contain a mix of volatile oils that may help to alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cuminum cyminum seeds at the right stage of maturity for optimal essential oil content.
Dry the seeds to a moisture level of 10% to prevent spoilage and bacterial growth.
Steam distill the dried seeds using a still to extract the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and store it in a cool dark place.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with difficulty eating because it improves blood flow to the brain, boosting cognitive function and memory. This can aid individuals with swallowing disorders or difficulty initiating meals due to mental fog. The oil's antioxidant properties may also help protect brain cells from damage, promoting a healthy appetite and swallowing mechanism. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory effects may soothe digestive issues, making it easier to eat and digest food. Regular use may lead to improved mealtime experiences.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These diterpene trilactones help alleviate difficulty eating by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which can aid in digestion and reduce discomfort.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the digestive system from damage and promote healing, which can ease eating difficulties.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, making it easier to eat and digest food.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used to make essential oils for difficulty eating due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help improve blood flow to the brain and digestive system.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make essential oils for difficulty eating because they contain flavonoids, which have neuroprotective effects and may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression related to eating disorders.

Roots: Ginkgo biloba roots are used to make essential oils for difficulty eating because they contain compounds that may help regulate digestive function and reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees and dry them in the shade for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a distillation apparatus at 100-110 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water in a condenser and measure 1-2 ml.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Label and date the ginkgo biloba essential oil bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils helps with difficulty eating because it contains bioactive compounds that support digestive health. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food. Additionally, Piper nigrum essential oils may increase saliva production, which can aid in the breakdown of food. Its anxiolytic properties may also help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with eating, promoting a more relaxed and comfortable eating experience. This can lead to improved digestion and reduced discomfort during meals.
remedy_constituents: Beta-Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene constituent has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eating disorders, making it easier to consume food.

Eugenol: As a phenolic compound, eugenol has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and improve appetite, making eating more manageable.

Sesquiterpene: Piper nigrum essential oils contain various sesquiterpenes, including alpha-pinene, which has been found to have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing stress and anxiety related to eating, and making the process more comfortable.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of piperine, which can aid digestion and relieve digestive issues.
Leaves: Used to make essential oils for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and promote healthy eating.
Fruits: Used to make essential oils for their carminative properties, which can help alleviate digestive discomfort, bloating, and other issues that may hinder eating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the piper nigrum berries when they are ripe and fully matured from the plant.
Dry the harvested berries in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried berries at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the distilled oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or residues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils helps with difficulty eating because it has a warming and invigorating effect on the digestive system. The aroma of this oil stimulates the brain to release digestive enzymes, improving appetite and reducing nausea. Its anti-inflammatory properties also soothe digestive issues such as bloating and cramps, making it easier to consume food. Additionally, Cinnamomum zeylanicum oil has a calming effect on the mind, reducing anxiety related to eating, thereby enhancing the overall digestive experience.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Acts as a natural anxiolytic and analgesic, helping to reduce anxiety and pain associated with eating difficulties.
Cinnamaldehyde: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate inflammation and discomfort in the digestive system, making eating more comfortable.
Caryophyllene: Shows potential in stimulating appetite and improving digestion, which can help individuals with eating difficulties by promoting a healthy appetite and gut function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils that help ease digestive issues, including difficulty eating, due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Barks: Barks are used to make essential oils that aid in digestion and alleviate discomfort during eating, thanks to their warm and aromatic properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make essential oils that help soothe digestive issues and promote a healthy appetite, benefiting from their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves and branches from mature plants during the peak oil content season.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a still at a temperature of 100-110°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and condense it into a liquid using a condenser coil for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or centrifugation to obtain 50g of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.
